brand.name: Hall Street Media (The Hall Street Journal)

summary: Hall Street Media publishes The Hall Street Journal, a free, ad/sponsorship-funded online publication and digital media brand covering money, health, faith, family, news, and culture. It serves upwardly mobile adults (25-45), primarily Black/African American professionals, who want one trusted, culturally resonant source that translates complex topics into plain language and builds a community that grows together.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation by getting a new subscriber to their first 'this was written for me' read fast, and learn which content pillar matters most to them.
campaigns.trigger: Newsletter signup / list join — send instantly (open rates drop ~50% if delayed).
campaigns.segment: All new subscribers; branch on the pillar they self-select (money / health / faith / family / news & culture).
campaigns.rationale: Every backbone flow is missing and discovery is social-driven, so the welcome is the first owned touch that proves the brand is different and captures the one preference (pillar) that powers all later personalization.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations, and deliver an immediate 'written for me' win by letting the reader self-select the pillar they came for.
emails.subject: Welcome to The Hall Street Journal — pick what you came for
emails.preview: Money, health, faith, or family — tell us where you want to grow first.
keyPoints: Affirm why they're here: one trusted source for money, health, faith, and family — in language that actually sounds like us, no jargon
Offer a one-tap self-select (money / health / faith / family / news & culture) that routes them to a flagship plain-English piece and tags them for future sends
Set the cadence and promise no fluff; invite a real reply, and link the most-loved finance explainer as the instant first win

campaigns.name: The Hall Street Journal Weekly
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build authority with a consistent, reliable weekly edition that drives clicks back to the site — the core engagement metric that funds an ad/sponsorship model.
campaigns.segment: All engaged subscribers; suppress contacts still in the welcome flow and route recently-dormant readers to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: For a free publication the newsletter IS the product and the revenue engine, and inconsistent cadence is the brand's #1 churn risk — a dependable, well-formatted weekly fixes the gap that hurt past retention.

emails.purpose: The flagship weekly digest template: one useful idea per pillar, a single primary CTA back to the site, and a recurring habit-building section.
emails.subject: This week in money, faith & family — the HSJ rundown
emails.preview: One plain-English money move, one faith note, one thing worth your time.
keyPoints: Lead with one practical, plain-English money or career move (with a standard 'not financial advice' note), then short hits across faith, family, and health
Recurring habit section ('This week's real-talk money tip') and dynamic blocks ordered by the reader's selected pillar; 80% value / 20% soft offer
Single clear primary CTA to read the week's feature on-site; warm community sign-off that invites replies and shares

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant subscribers and clean the list to protect sender reputation before pouring budget into growth.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold — 60-90 days with no opens/clicks.
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers (no engagement in 60+ days), separated from any never-engaged signups.
campaigns.rationale: A history of irregular publishing likely left a tail of lapsed readers; reclaiming 5-15% of them is fast, cheap, and improves deliverability for every other campaign before growth spend ramps.

emails.purpose: Open the win-back with a warm 'we miss you', remind them of the core value, and surface what they've missed.
emails.subject: Did we lose you? Your seat at the table's still here
emails.preview: We've been doing the work — here's what you've missed.
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt 'we've been thinking about you' tone that reaffirms the community and the brand's reason for being
Show 2-3 of the strongest recent pieces (money, faith, family) as proof the cadence and quality are back and consistent
Single low-friction CTA to read one piece; set up the later sunset email so non-responders can be suppressed to protect deliverability

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Convert social followers and casual visitors into committed subscribers by delivering a gated guide and nurturing them toward becoming regular, engaged readers.
campaigns.trigger: Download of a gated lead magnet (e.g., a plain-English money guide for the upwardly mobile).
campaigns.segment: New leads from the magnet; branch the middle of the sequence by the magnet topic (money / faith & family / health).
campaigns.rationale: Discovery is almost entirely social today with no downloadable assets; a signature gated guide gives the brand a top-of-funnel capture engine that turns scattered followers into owned, addressable subscribers.

emails.purpose: Deliver the requested guide and frame the real problem it solves to set up the rest of the nurture.
emails.subject: Your money guide's inside — let's talk about the real problem
emails.preview: Why 'just budget better' has never worked for us — and what does.
keyPoints: Deliver the download immediately and name the deeper problem: generic finance advice that ignores our reality and starting point
Frame HSJ as the source that explains money (and the rest of a well-rounded life) in language that sounds like us, with a 'not financial advice' note
Tease the next emails (the framework, then proof) and invite a reply with the one money question they most want answered

campaigns.name: Referral / Advocacy
campaigns.objective: Turn engaged readers into the brand's primary growth channel by making sharing The Journal natural at moments of value.
campaigns.trigger: Value moment — a reader clicks/reads several pieces, finishes a guide, or hits an engagement milestone.
campaigns.segment: Highly engaged subscribers and advocates (repeat clickers, guide finishers).
campaigns.rationale: This is a community-minded, word-of-mouth-driven brand with no paid tier to expand, so advocacy — not renewal — is the natural retention-stage growth lever and amplifies the social momentum that already drives signups.

emails.purpose: Catch the reader at a value high and ask them to bring someone else into the community.
emails.subject: Know someone who needs to hear this? Bring them in
emails.preview: The Journal grows by word of mouth — share it with someone climbing too.
keyPoints: Acknowledge they've been getting real value lately and tie it to the mission of developing and rising together as a community
Make sharing one tap: a personal forward, a ready-to-post social link, and a simple 'invite a friend to subscribe' link
Frame the ask around the friend's benefit ('someone who could use plain-talk money and life advice'), not a transactional reward
